hypotheses.statement: A precursor-based small-molecule inhibitor selectively passivates the non-growth surface while surface grows the target film on the growth surface, achieving >=90% selectivity at 10 nm for passivate a-SiN, grow SiOx-on-a-SiO2 to 90% selectivity at 10 nm.
hypotheses.rationale: Derived from co-occurrence of precursor and surface in the knowledge graph.

supporting_evidence.statement: Literature links precursor to passivate a-SiN, grow SiOx-on-a-SiO2 to 90% selectivity at 10 nm.

contradicting_evidence.statement: Some sources do not isolate precursor's specific effect.

assumptions: precursor is measurable in the relevant system.

hypotheses.statement: Chemisorption of surface on the non-growth surface blocks growth precursor adsorption, delaying nucleation enough to reach the selectivity target for passivate a-SiN, grow SiOx-on-a-SiO2 to 90% selectivity at 10 nm.
hypotheses.rationale: Derived from co-occurrence of surface and growth in the knowledge graph.

supporting_evidence.statement: Literature links surface to passivate a-SiN, grow SiOx-on-a-SiO2 to 90% selectivity at 10 nm.

contradicting_evidence.statement: Some sources do not isolate surface's specific effect.

assumptions: surface is measurable in the relevant system.

hypotheses.statement: growth and area-selective form a compatible inhibitor/precursor pair whose differential adsorption drives area-selective growth in passivate a-SiN, grow SiOx-on-a-SiO2 to 90% selectivity at 10 nm.
hypotheses.rationale: Derived from co-occurrence of growth and area-selective in the knowledge graph.

hypotheses.statement: The selectivity in passivate a-SiN, grow SiOx-on-a-SiO2 to 90% selectivity at 10 nm is governed by the differential blocking coverage of area-selective between the growth and non-growth surfaces rather than by selectivity alone.
hypotheses.rationale: Derived from co-occurrence of area-selective and selectivity in the knowledge graph.
